Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Micro-and meso-structures and their influence on mechanical properties of selectively laser melted Ti-6Al-4V

Composition_Info.Note: Commercial Ti-6Al-4V ELI grade powder; balance Ti

Process_Text.original: Selective laser melting (SLM) using EOSINT M 280 with Yb:YAG fiber laser in argon atmosphere; laser power 280 W, scan speed 1200 mm/s, scan spacing 0.14 mm, layer thickness 30 um, scan rotation 90 deg; volumetric energy density 55.6 J/mm3; post-process stress relief annealing at 650 degC for 3 h
Process_Text.simplified: SLM (EOSINT M 280, Yb:YAG); 280 W, 1200 mm/s, 0.14 mm spacing, 30 um layer, 90 deg rotation; 55.6 J/mm3; stress relieved 650 degC/3h

Microstructure_Text.original: Columnar prior beta grains spanning entire height of coupon in S-plane; square cross-section in B-plane with side length ~140 um equal to scan spacing; fine acicular alpha/alpha' laths within prior beta grains; alpha phase decorating prior beta grain boundaries; laths oriented at ~45 deg to BD due to Burger's orientation relationship; epitaxial growth along <001>_beta direction normal to deposited layers
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Columnar prior beta grains with square cross-section ~140 um; fine acicular alpha/alpha' lath structure; alpha at prior beta boundaries; epitaxial columnar growth

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 0.001 /s; displacement control 0.006 mm/s
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8 dog-bone; 6 mm gage length; 2x0.5 mm2 cross-section
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 0.001 /s
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8 dog-bone; 6 mm gage length; 2x0.5 mm2 cross-section
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 0.001 /s
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8 dog-bone; 6 mm gage length; 2x0.5 mm2 cross-section
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD); minimum ductility 5% aerospace requirement satisfied

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; rotating bending fatigue (RBF); minimum 1e7 cycles survival
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Hourglass shaped; gauge length 22 mm; minimum diameter ~5 mm; mirror polished
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction only (cylindrical block geometry); FS/sigma_U = 0.28

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; plane strain conditions confirmed per ASTM E399-12
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Chevron notch half-sized C(T) specimen; 12 mm thick, 24 mm wide; a/W ~0.45-0.55
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; load shedding method per ASTM E647-15; R=0.1; 10 Hz sinusoidal; da/dN threshold 2e-9 m/cycle
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Chevron notch half-sized C(T) specimen; 12 mm thick, 24 mm wide; precracked to a/W ~0.45-0.55
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; steady state FCG regime; R=0.1; 10 Hz sinusoidal
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Chevron notch half-sized C(T) specimen; precracked to a/W ~0.45-0.55
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD); Paris equation: da/dN = C(DeltaK)^m

Composition_Info.Note: Commercial Ti-6Al-4V ELI grade powder; balance Ti

Process_Text.original: Selective laser melting (SLM) using EOSINT M 280 with Yb:YAG fiber laser in argon atmosphere; laser power 280 W, scan speed 1200 mm/s, scan spacing 0.14 mm, layer thickness 30 um, scan rotation 67 deg; volumetric energy density 55.6 J/mm3; post-process stress relief annealing at 650 degC for 3 h
Process_Text.simplified: SLM (EOSINT M 280, Yb:YAG); 280 W, 1200 mm/s, 0.14 mm spacing, 30 um layer, 67 deg rotation; 55.6 J/mm3; stress relieved 650 degC/3h

Microstructure_Text.original: Equiaxed prior beta grains in B-plane with average diameter ~140 um (equal to scan spacing); irregular/jagged prior beta grain boundaries in S-plane due to 67 deg mismatch between successive laser tracks; melt pool overlap causes some columnar character but walls are discontinuous; fine acicular alpha/alpha' laths within prior beta grains; alpha phase decorating prior beta grain boundaries; laths oriented at ~45 deg to BD
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Near-equiaxed prior beta grains ~140 um in B-plane; irregular/discontinuous boundaries in S-plane; fine acicular alpha/alpha' lath structure; 67 deg rotation breaks columnar structure

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 0.001 /s; displacement control 0.006 mm/s
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8 dog-bone; 6 mm gage length; 2x0.5 mm2 cross-section
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD); no anisotropy (same as S3067)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 0.001 /s
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8 dog-bone; 6 mm gage length; 2x0.5 mm2 cross-section
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 0.001 /s
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8 dog-bone; 6 mm gage length; 2x0.5 mm2 cross-section
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD); 67 deg rotation improves ductility vs 90 deg; minimum ductility 5% aerospace requirement satisfied

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; rotating bending fatigue (RBF); minimum 1e7 cycles survival
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Hourglass shaped; gauge length 22 mm; minimum diameter ~5 mm; mirror polished
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction only (cylindrical block geometry); FS/sigma_U = 0.29; similar to 3090 despite different mesostructure

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; plane strain conditions confirmed per ASTM E399-12
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Chevron notch half-sized C(T) specimen; 12 mm thick, 24 mm wide; a/W ~0.45-0.55
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD); shows some anisotropy with S direction

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; load shedding method per ASTM E647-15; R=0.1; 10 Hz sinusoidal; da/dN threshold 2e-9 m/cycle
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Chevron notch half-sized C(T) specimen; 12 mm thick, 24 mm wide; precracked to a/W ~0.45-0.55
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; steady state FCG regime; R=0.1; 10 Hz sinusoidal
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Chevron notch half-sized C(T) specimen; precracked to a/W ~0.45-0.55
Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD); Paris equation: da/dN = C(DeltaK)^m; higher m indicates lower FCG resistance

Composition_Info.Note: Commercial Ti-6Al-4V ELI grade powder; balance Ti

Process_Text.original: Selective laser melting (SLM) using EOSINT M 280 with Yb:YAG fiber laser in argon atmosphere; laser power 340 W, scan speed 1250 mm/s, scan spacing 0.12 mm, layer thickness 60 um, scan rotation 90 deg; volumetric energy density 37.8 J/mm3; post-process stress relief annealing at 650 degC for 3 h
Process_Text.simplified: SLM (EOSINT M 280, Yb:YAG); 340 W, 1250 mm/s, 0.12 mm spacing, 60 um layer, 90 deg rotation; 37.8 J/mm3; stress relieved 650 degC/3h

Microstructure_Text.original: Square cross-section prior beta grains in B-plane with side length ~120 um (reflects reduced scan spacing); shorter melt pool depth prevents epitaxial growth along BD; shortened vertical columnar grains in S-plane due to melt pool boundaries; boundaries are close to linear but discontinuous; fine acicular alpha/alpha' laths within prior beta grains; alpha phase decorating prior beta grain boundaries; laths oriented at ~45 deg to BD
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Square prior beta grains ~120 um in B-plane; shortened columnar grains in S-plane (melt pool boundaries); fine acicular alpha/alpha' lath structure; epitaxy broken due to lower energy density

Properties_Info.Note: Build direction (parallel to BD); nearly isotropic (~3% difference from S direction)

Properties_Info.Note: Build direction only (cylindrical block geometry); FS/sigma_U = 0.37; substantially higher than 30 um layer thickness samples; comparable to conventionally manufactured Ti64

Process_Text.original: Selective laser melting (SLM) using EOSINT M 280 with Yb:YAG fiber laser in argon atmosphere; laser power 340 W, scan speed 1250 mm/s, scan spacing 0.12 mm, layer thickness 60 um, scan rotation 67 deg; volumetric energy density 37.8 J/mm3; post-process stress relief annealing at 650 degC for 3 h
Process_Text.simplified: SLM (EOSINT M 280, Yb:YAG); 340 W, 1250 mm/s, 0.12 mm spacing, 60 um layer, 67 deg rotation; 37.8 J/mm3; stress relieved 650 degC/3h

Microstructure_Text.original: Completely equiaxed prior beta grains in B-plane (optimum mesostructure); irregular and short grain boundaries in S-plane; 67 deg rotation combined with reduced energy density eliminates epitaxial growth; fine acicular alpha/alpha' laths within prior beta grains; alpha phase decorating prior beta grain boundaries; 60 um layer thickness gives highest ductility
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fully equiaxed prior beta grains (optimum mesostructure); irregular/short boundaries in S-plane; fine acicular alpha/alpha' lath structure; best combination of strength and ductility

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.Prior_Beta_Grain_Size_S_plane_um: irregular/short (67 deg mismatch + melt pool boundaries)

Process_Text.original: Selective laser melting (SLM) using EOSINT M 280 with Yb:YAG fiber laser in argon atmosphere; laser power 340 W, scan speed 1250 mm/s, scan spacing 0.12 mm, layer thickness 60 um, scan rotation 67 deg; volumetric energy density 37.8 J/mm3; post-process stress relief annealing at 650 degC for 3 h
Process_Text.simplified: SLM (EOSINT M 280, Yb:YAG); 340 W, 1250 mm/s, 0.12 mm spacing, 60 um layer, 67 deg rotation; 37.8 J/mm3; stress relieved 650 degC/3h

Microstructure_Text.original: Completely equiaxed prior beta grains in B-plane (optimum mesostructure); irregular and short grain boundaries in S-plane; 67 deg rotation combined with reduced energy density eliminates epitaxial growth; fine acicular alpha/alpha' laths within prior beta grains; alpha phase decorating prior beta grain boundaries; corrugated fracture surface due to equiaxed prior beta grains in S-plane; 60 um layer thickness gives highest ductility
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fully equiaxed prior beta grains (optimum mesostructure); irregular/short boundaries in S-plane; fine acicular alpha/alpha' lath structure; corrugated fracture surface indicating tortuous crack path; best combination of strength and ductility
